Document:
# Chems & Addiction
[textlink="View Full Wiki Page" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Chems%20%26%20Addiction"/]
◈ Chems & Addiction ◈ Wasteland Pharmacology ◈
The Wasteland offers a range of chemical substances - some that save lives, others that ruin them. Understanding what each chem does, how it affects the body, and [bold]what happens when you stop taking it[/bold] is the difference between using a tool and becoming a slave to one.
## Stimpaks
The backbone of Wasteland medicine. Stimpaks are injectable serums that rapidly accelerate the body's healing. Multiple variants exist with different potency and risk.
[bold]Item[/bold] [bold]Effect[/bold] [bold]Overdose[/bold] [bold]Notes[/bold] Stimpak Heals Brute, Burn, and Airloss at -2.5 per tick. Stops bleeding (-2). Grants recovery drug status (can stand from crit). 30u: bonus healing but +0.3 poison/tick. 50u: +1.5 poison/tick (pushes to crit, cannot kill). Primary healing item. Crafted from Broc Flower + Xander Root + Empty Syringe + Bloodpack. [bold]Dirty Stimpak[/bold] Heals Brute, Burn, and Airloss at -1.5 per tick. Applies +0.1 radiation per tick. Stops bleeding (-1). 30u: weaker bonus healing but +0.2 poison/tick. 50u: +1.0 poison/tick. Contaminated variant. Weaker than a clean stimpak but still effective. Found in loot or purchased cheaply. Super Stimpak Heals Brute, Burn, and Airloss at -6.125 per tick. Stops bleeding (-3). Grants recovery drug status. 30u: bonus healing but +4.5 poison and +0.75 cellular damage per tick. Also halves movement speed. Extremely powerful. Taking two at once is near-guaranteed crit. Cellular damage is [bold]permanent[/bold]. Found in elite medical kits or high-value loot.
## Other Medical Items
Non-stimpak healing items and medical supplies.
[bold]Item[/bold] [bold]Effect[/bold] [bold]Notes[/bold] Healing Powder Heals Brute and Burn at -1.5 per tick. Causes vision blur (haze overlay). Stops bleeding (-0.5). Slow metabolism (0.1 rate). Tribal remedy crafted from Broc Flower + Xander Root. Vomiting at 30u. Standard issue for Legion and tribal roles. Healing Poultice Heals Brute and Burn at -3.0 per tick. Stops bleeding (-1.0). Causes jitter. Faster metabolism (0.2 rate). Stronger than healing powder. Crafted from Broc Flower + Xander Root + Cave Fungus + Agave Leaf. Vomiting at 25u. RadAway Removes accumulated radiation damage. Critical for survival in irradiated areas. Non-addictive. [bold]Antidote[/bold] Neutralizes poison damage and effects. Counter to poisoning.
## Combat Chems
Combat chems provide powerful temporary bonuses but carry [bold]addiction risk[/bold]. Continued use builds dependency. Stopping use triggers [bold]withdrawal[/bold].
[bold]Chem[/bold] [bold]Effect[/bold] [bold]Overdose[/bold] [bold]Withdrawal[/bold] Jet +10% walk and sprint speed. Removes stun/knockdown effects. 30+ units: asphyxiation Speed penalty (0.80×), stamina drain 5.0 [bold]Turbo[/bold] +20% walk and sprint speed. Drunk effect. Removes stun. 15+ units: asphyxiation Speed penalty (0.70×), stamina drain 7.0 [bold]Psycho[/bold] +2 melee strength. Drunk effect. 30+ units: asphyxiation Brute damage 0.2/tick, stamina drain 6.0 [bold]Buffout[/bold] +2 strength. Minor brute healing (-0.3). 30+ units: asphyxiation Brute damage 0.2/tick, stamina drain 8.0 [bold]Mentats[/bold] Intelligence and perception boost. - Stamina drain (moderate) [bold]Cateye[/bold] Night vision (5 seconds per dose). - Stamina drain 2.0 [bold]Daytripper[/bold] Visual hallucinations, rainbow vision. - Speed penalty (0.90×), stamina drain 4.0 [bold]Buffjet[/bold] Combined Buffout + Jet effects. Combined Combined withdrawal effects [bold]Bufftats[/bold] Combined Mentats + Buffout effects. Combined Combined withdrawal effects
[bold]Overdose rule:[/bold] Overdose can push you to [bold]critical but never kill you[/bold] directly. Damage scales with excess chemical concentration (0.05 per excess unit).
## Addiction System
Using combat chems builds chemical dependency over time. When a chem wears off, [bold]withdrawal[/bold] begins.
Withdrawal severity depends on how long ago the chem wore off:
[bold]Tier[/bold] [bold]Time Remaining[/bold] [bold]Symptoms[/bold] Severe > 120 seconds Maximum speed penalty, stamina drain, health damage, mood effects [bold]Moderate[/bold] 60 – 120 seconds Reduced penalties, still noticeable performance impact [bold]Mild[/bold] 15 – 60 seconds Minor penalties, the worst has passed [bold]Fading[/bold] < 15 seconds Nearly clean. Hold on.
Withdrawal effects tick every [bold]15 seconds[/bold] and include:
[bold]Speed penalties[/bold] - movement speed reduced (per-drug, 0.70× to 0.90×)
[bold]Stamina drain[/bold] - stamina depletes faster (2.0 to 8.0 per tick)
[bold]Health damage[/bold] - some drugs cause direct brute or burn damage during withdrawal
[bold]Mood effects[/bold] - negative mood modifiers until clean
[bold]Chem[/bold] [bold]Recipe[/bold] [bold]Notes[/bold] [bold]Jet[/bold] Ammonia 1 + Abraxo 1 (heated to 374°C) Requires heat source [bold]Psycho[/bold] Bloatfly Acid 1 + Broc Extract 2 + Blood 1 + Antiseptic 2 Complex recipe, rare ingredients [bold]Turbo[/bold] Cazador Acid 1 + Broc Extract 1 + Jet 1 + Turpentine 1 Requires Jet as base ingredient [bold]Mentats[/bold] Abraxo 1 + Lead 1 Simple recipe [bold]Buffjet[/bold] Jet 1 + Buffout 1 Combination drug [bold]Bufftats[/bold] Mentats 1 + Buffout 1 Combination drug [bold]Hydra[/bold] Scorpion Tail 1 + Fungus Extract 1 + Nightstalker Blood 1 Healing/stat hybrid [bold]RadAway[/bold] Fungus Extract (Rad) 1 + Antiseptic 2 + Water 2 Medical, non-addictive [bold]Stimpak[/bold] Broc Extract 1 + Xander Extract 1 + Blood 1 Medical, non-addictive
